column1: # ZAP Scan Rules Configuration
# Book Reference: Chapter 3, Section 3.1.3.2
# FORMAT:
#   <rule_id>  <action>  # <description>
# ACTIONS:
#   FAIL   - Finding at this rule causes the scan to fail (exit code 1)
#   WARN   - Finding is reported as a warning but does not fail the scan
#   IGNORE - Finding is suppressed entirely (use for confirmed false positives)
#   INFO   - Finding is reported at informational level only
# FINDING RULE IDs:
#   Full list: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/alerts/
# USAGE:
#   Copy this file to .zap/rules.tsv in your repository root.
#   Reference it in your ZAP scan command:
#     zap-baseline.py -t https://staging.yourapp.com -c .zap/rules.tsv
# CRITICAL - Always fail the build
# These represent severe vulnerabilities that must be fixed before deployment
# HIGH - Fail the build
# Serious vulnerabilities that should block deployment

column3: # Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL
# Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in HTTP Referrer Header
# User Controllable HTML Element Attribute (Potential XSS)
# Server Leaks Version Information via "Server" HTTP Response Header
# Server Leaks Information via "X-Powered-By" HTTP Response Header
# Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
# HTTP to HTTPS Insecure Transition in Form Post
# HTTPS to HTTP Insecure Transition in Form Post
# Big Redirect Detected (Potential Sensitive Information Leak)

column1: # MEDIUM - Warn but do not fail
# Should be reviewed and fixed but won't block deployment
# Adjust to FAIL once your team has addressed the backlog

column3: # Incomplete or No Cache-control Header Set
# Web Browser XSS Protection Not Enabled

column1: # LOW - Informational only
# Low-risk findings worth tracking but not blocking

column3: # Incomplete or No Cache-control Header Set
# Insufficient Site Isolation Against Spectre Vulnerability

column1: # IGNORE - Confirmed false positives for this application
# Uncomment and add rule IDs for confirmed false positives in your application.
# Always add a comment explaining WHY the rule is ignored.
# Example:
# 10202  IGNORE  # Anti-CSRF tokens not needed - API uses SameSite=Strict cookies
#                # and all state-changing requests require Authorization header
# 10054  IGNORE  # SameSite cookie attribute - handled by reverse proxy, not app
